excerpt: The intellect acts benevolently toward the self, enabling it to transform back to its primordial state and return to the spiritual realm.
The muscles and sensory organs allow human movement and perception through the soul's command.
source_location_hint: treatment of bodily faculties and soul

excerpt: The fetus in the womb passes through three stages of darkness and confinement before birth into the world.
source_location_hint: parallels between womb and physical world

excerpt: The relationship between partial and whole souls follows the logic of numerical operations and mutual influence.
source_location_hint: doctrine of degrees of intellect and soul

excerpt: The soul cannot perceive the realities of the Hereafter while confined to the body, just as the fetus cannot perceive the world.
The intellect commands what is right and forbids what is wrong; the self enslaved to lust disobeys and leads to corruption.
The perfected soul, having governed itself and its household, ascends to divine governance and heavenly reward.
source_location_hint: stages of soul's ascent through governance

excerpt: I am in the grave, and Sayyidna Sulayman is in the grave, because all the knowledge of the Messenger of Allah is buried in Ali.
source_location_hint: Opening section on grave and soul-body separation

excerpt: The body is a corpse that the soul carries upon its shoulders. The soul does not grow weary under this burden—only when it finally buries the body in the earth does it find relief.
source_work: Esoteric spiritual teachings on nafs and jasm

excerpt: The soul is sick, and this is precisely why it has entered this world. What are these religions but remedies and prescriptions for the cure of the soul's maladies?
source_work: Taʾwil al-Daʿaʾim or similar Ismaili soteriological text
source_authority: Fatimid-era doctrine on taʾwīl and spiritual healing
source_location_hint: Religious law as medicine for the soul

excerpt: For a believer, even to command what is right and forbid what is wrong is a great virtue, even as they themselves practice that good.
source_location_hint: Doctrine of amr bi-l-maʿrūf wa-l-nahī ʿan al-munkar

excerpt: The best of people is one who benefits the people. Make this blessed decree the goal of your life.
